test_cases.title: Notification is successfully delivered to a valid user
preconditions: A valid user account exists in the system
The user has a registered notification channel (e.g., email, in-app, or push)
steps: Trigger a notification event for the target user
Observe the notification delivery to the user's registered channel
test_cases.expected_result: The user receives the notification on their registered channel within an observable timeframe

test_cases.title: Notification is not sent when no valid user is specified
preconditions: No valid user identifier is provided or the user does not exist in the system
steps: Trigger a notification event with an invalid or non-existent user identifier
test_cases.expected_result: The system does not send a notification and returns an error or logs a failure indicating the user was not found

test_cases.title: Notification is not sent to an unauthorized or deactivated user
preconditions: A user account exists but is deactivated or suspended in the system
steps: Trigger a notification event targeting the deactivated user
Observe whether a notification is sent
test_cases.expected_result: The system does not deliver the notification to the deactivated user

test_cases.title: Notification is not sent when notification channel is missing or invalid
preconditions: The user has no registered or valid notification channel configured
steps: Trigger a notification event for the target user who has no notification channel
test_cases.expected_result: The system does not send a notification and logs or reports the failure due to missing notification channel

test_cases.title: Notification content is not exposed to unintended users
preconditions: At least two valid user accounts exist in the system
steps: Trigger a notification event intended for User A
Observe whether User B receives or can access the notification meant for User A
test_cases.expected_result: User B does not receive and cannot access the notification intended for User A

assumptions: A notification channel exists (e.g., email, push notification, or in-app message), though the specific channel type is not specified in the requirement
The system has a mechanism to identify the target user for a notification
Deactivated or suspended users are assumed to be a valid system state
The system logs notification delivery outcomes (success or failure)
notes: REQ-009 is critically underspecified. The following aspects are ambiguous or missing: (1) What types of notifications are sent (transactional, marketing, system alerts, etc.); (2) What triggers a notification (user action, scheduled event, admin action, etc.); (3) Which delivery channels are supported (email, SMS, push, in-app); (4) Whether notification delivery is synchronous or asynchronous; (5) Whether users can opt out of notifications; (6) Expected delivery SLA or timing guarantees; (7) Retry behavior on delivery failure. Test cases are intentionally generic due to these gaps. The requirement should be refined before comprehensive test coverage can be achieved.
